prompt: Create a photorealistic product-display image of exactly 4 collectible anime idol figurines posed inside a dark glass display cabinet, arranged in a 2-by-2 grid with black metal dividers between the four compartments. Use a cinematic warm spotlight from above in each compartment, glossy reflections on the glass, shallow depth of field, and a premium museum-diorama look. Each figurine sits or kneels on a circular polished wooden pedestal, with a softly blurred doll-like face and highly detailed glossy resin skin, hair, clothing, ribbons, gloves, stockings, and metallic accessories. The overall theme is luxury fantasy idol figures in antique glass cases. Include exactly 4 figurines: top left is a purple-haired twin-tail idol in a white, black, and lavender frilled stage outfit, seated sideways with one hand on the pedestal and the other arm extended outward, wearing thigh-high stockings and garter details; top right is a blue-haired curled-twin-tail idol in a dark navy plaid school-uniform-inspired outfit, kneeling on the pedestal with one arm raised in a peace-sign gesture, wearing gloves and layered ribbons; bottom left is a pink-haired pastel idol in a pale pink frilly dress, seated with one leg folded and one hand touching her head, with blue-and-pink stockings and delicate bows; bottom right is a short white-and-blue-haired idol in a white, silver, and cyan outfit with small wing-like shoulder details and a star hair ornament, seated cross-legged while pointing toward the viewer. Surround the figures with antique props inside the cases: framed art prints, small books, vintage cameras, glass bottles, dried flowers in vases, a brass armillary sphere, a lantern with a candle, and a large antique clock. Keep the environment dark brown and black with warm amber highlights, realistic glass reflections, subtle dust and scratches, and a high-end collectible photography style. Use 3:2 horizontal, warm moody cabinet spotlighting, dark walnut, black metal, amber light, lavender, navy, pink, cyan, and ultra-realistic resin figure photography. No text, no watermark, no extra figurines, no human hands, no packaging boxes.
